title: Nonin Onyx II 9590 Pulse Oximeter - Sensor Not Detecting or Low Perfusion Errors
description: Addresses failure to detect signal or low perfusion readings due to positioning, patient condition, battery, or environmental factors.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from active patient use if readings are unreliable. Use an alternate monitoring method if needed.

steps.title: Verify Device Power and Startup Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the device and observe the display. Confirm the unit powers on normally, check for low battery indicator, and observe if display activates fully.

steps.title: Check Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect and replace batteries if needed. Open battery compartment, verify correct battery orientation, and replace with fresh, high-quality batteries.

steps.title: Verify Proper Finger Placement
steps.instructions: Ensure the patient’s finger is correctly positioned in the device. Insert finger fully into the clip, align fingernail facing upward, and ensure no movement during reading.

steps.title: Assess Patient Condition (Perfusion)
steps.instructions: Evaluate for factors affecting circulation. Check for cold extremities, look for poor capillary refill, and warm the finger if needed.

steps.title: Remove External Interference
steps.instructions: Check for anything that may block or distort the sensor signal. Remove nail polish or artificial nails, clean finger if soiled, and avoid bright ambient light.

steps.title: Test on Known Good Subject
steps.instructions: Verify device function using a healthy individual. Place device on yourself or another staff member and confirm consistent SpO2 and pulse readings.

steps.title: Inspect Device for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Check the sensor clip and internal surfaces. Look for cracked housing or loose hinge and inspect internal emitter/detector area for debris or damage.

CCR.Complaint: Pulse oximeter not detecting signal and showing low perfusion on patient.
CCR.Cause: Weak batteries and improper finger placement identified during testing.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced batteries and corrected finger positioning; device returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Device tested on known good subject
Presence of nail polish or interference

title: Nonin Onyx II 9590 Pulse Oximeter - Battery Not Charging or Power Failure
description: Device will not power on, shuts off unexpectedly, or shows no response due to battery depletion or charging-related issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from patient use if it cannot power on or maintain operation. Use an alternate pulse oximeter. Expected: Patient monitoring continues safely Why it matters: Intermittent or no readings can lead to missed hypoxia

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to power on the device and observe behavior. Check for: No display or screen activity, Device powers on briefly then shuts off, No response when button is pressed Expected: Clear confirmation of power-related failure Why it matters: Confirms true power issue vs user operation error

steps.title: Check Battery Installation and Type
steps.instructions: Open the battery compartment and verify batteries are installed correctly. Action: Confirm polarity (+/- orientation), Ensure correct battery type (typically AAA alkaline), Check for loose or improperly seated batteries Expected: Properly installed batteries allow device to power on Why it matters: Incorrect installation is a common cause of failure

steps.title: Replace Batteries with Known Good Set
steps.instructions: Remove existing batteries and install a new set of high-quality alkaline batteries. Action: Do not reuse partially depleted batteries, Avoid mixing old and new batteries Expected: Device powers on normally Why it matters: Low or depleted batteries are the most common cause of power issues

steps.title: Inspect Battery Contacts
steps.instructions: Examine the battery compartment for contact issues. Check for: Corrosion or residue on contacts, Bent or recessed terminals, Dirt or debris preventing contact Action: Clean lightly with approved method if needed, Ensure contacts are intact and making firm connection Expected: Clean, intact contacts restore power path Why it matters: Poor contact prevents proper power delivery

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage or Moisture Exposure
steps.instructions: Inspect the device housing and battery compartment. Look for: Cracks or damage near battery area, Signs of fluid ingress, Warping or discoloration Expected: No visible damage affecting power system Why it matters: Internal damage can interrupt power flow

steps.title: Test Power Button Function
steps.instructions: Press and hold the power button firmly. Observe: Any flicker or intermittent response, Lack of tactile feedback Expected: Consistent response when pressing button Why it matters: Worn or failed button can mimic power failure

steps.title: Attempt Multiple Power Cycles
steps.instructions: With fresh batteries installed, attempt to power cycle the unit several times. Expected: Device consistently powers on Why it matters: Rules out intermittent electrical contact issues

CCR.Complaint: Pulse oximeter would not power on during patient use.
CCR.Cause: Batteries depleted and battery contacts slightly corroded.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced batteries and cleaned contacts; device powered on and passed functional check.

helpfulDetails: Device powered on successfully or not
Any signs of corrosion or damage
Final device status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: Nonin Onyx II 9590 Pulse Oximeter - Display or Button Failure
description: Unresponsive buttons, blank or dim display, or inability to power on due to external issues like batteries, contacts, or physical damage.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from active patient use. Use a known working pulse oximeter for monitoring. Expected: Patient is safely monitored with alternate equipment Why it matters: Inaccurate or unavailable SpO₂ readings can impact clinical decisions

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Press the power button and observe: Does the display turn on at all? Are buttons responsive or completely unresponsive? Is the display dim, flickering, or blank? Expected: Clear confirmation of display or button failure Why it matters: Differentiates between power, display, or input-related issues

steps.title: Check Battery Condition (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Open the battery compartment and inspect: Ensure batteries are installed correctly Replace with known good batteries Check for corrosion or residue on contacts Expected: Device powers on with clear display and responsive buttons Why it matters: Low or depleted batteries commonly cause display and input failures If resolved, return device to service.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Contacts
steps.instructions: Look inside the battery compartment: Check for bent, dirty, or corroded contacts Clean contacts if needed using approved method Expected: Proper electrical connection restored Why it matters: Poor contact can prevent sufficient power from reaching the device If resolved, return device to service.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the unit: Cracked display window Worn or stuck buttons Signs of fluid ingress or contamination Expected: No visible damage affecting usability Why it matters: Physical damage can directly cause display or button failure If damage is found, remove from service.

steps.title: Perform a Power Reset
steps.instructions: Remove batteries completely: Wait 30–60 seconds Reinstall batteries and power on Expected: Device reinitializes normally Why it matters: Clears minor electronic glitches or lockups If resolved, return device to service.

steps.title: Test Button Functionality
steps.instructions: Press each button individually: Confirm tactile response Observe if display changes or reacts Expected: Buttons respond consistently Why it matters: Identifies if failure is isolated to specific buttons or full keypad

CCR.Complaint: Pulse oximeter display not turning on and buttons unresponsive. Buttons intermittently unresponsive on Onyx II 9590.
CCR.Cause: Depleted batteries preventing device startup. Physical wear and internal keypad failure.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced batteries and verified normal display and button function. Removed from service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Batteries replaced with known good set
Presence of physical damage or contamination
Device final status (returned to service or sent for repair)

title: Nonin Onyx II 9590 Pulse Oximeter - SpO2 Reading Inaccuracy
description: Troubleshooting inaccurate or inconsistent SpO2 readings caused by placement, perfusion, motion, or environmental issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If readings are being used for clinical decisions, verify with another pulse oximeter or monitoring device.

steps.title: Verify Proper Finger Placement
steps.instructions: Ensure the patient’s finger is fully inserted into the device with the nail facing upward toward the sensor. Reposition finger fully into the clip and ensure proper alignment of emitter and detector.

steps.title: Check for Motion Artifact
steps.instructions: Observe if the patient is moving their hand or finger during measurement. Ask patient to remain still and support the hand if needed.

steps.title: Assess Perfusion (Circulation Issues)
steps.instructions: Check for signs of poor circulation such as cold fingers, low blood pressure, or vasoconstriction. Warm the patient’s hand and try a different finger.

steps.title: Inspect for External Interference
steps.instructions: Remove nail polish or artificial nails and check for dirt, dried blood, or debris in the sensor area.

steps.title: Evaluate Ambient Light Conditions
steps.instructions: Shield the device from bright overhead lights or sunlight to reduce interference.

steps.title: Check Battery Status
steps.instructions: Replace batteries with known good ones to ensure proper device performance.

steps.title: Compare with Known Good Device
steps.instructions: Cross-check readings with another calibrated pulse oximeter to confirm device-specific issues.

CCR.Complaint: Pulse oximeter giving inconsistent and inaccurate SpO2 readings compared to other devices.
CCR.Cause: Poor signal quality due to internal sensor degradation after external factors were ruled out.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service and sent for further evaluation and repair.

title: Nonin Onyx II 9590 Pulse Oximeter - Probe or Clip Damage
description: Troubleshooting physical clip or probe damage causing poor fit or inaccurate readings.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the device from use if readings are unreliable or inconsistent. Use an alternate pulse oximeter for patient monitoring.

steps.title: Perform Visual Inspection of Clip and Sensor Area
steps.instructions: Inspect for cracks or broken plastic, loose hinge or weak spring tension, and misaligned sensor pads.

steps.title: Check Sensor Window Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect for dirt, debris, residue, scratches, or clouding. Clean gently with approved wipes if contaminated.

steps.title: Verify Clip Tension and Finger Fit
steps.instructions: Place on a test finger, ensure the clip closes fully, and confirm consistent pressure across the finger.

steps.title: Test Device Functionality
steps.instructions: Power on and apply to a known good subject. Observe SpO₂ and pulse readings for stability and consistency.

steps.title: Check for Housing Separation or Structural Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect for separation at seams, internal movement, and signs of impact damage.

steps.title: Compare with Known Good Device (If Available)
steps.instructions: Test the same patient with another pulse oximeter to compare readings.

CCR.Complaint: Pulse oximeter not staying on finger and giving inconsistent readings.
CCR.Cause: Clip hinge weakened and sensor alignment compromised due to physical wear.
CCR.Resolution: Removed device from service and sent for repair evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Condition of clip (cracked, loose, broken hinge)
Final device status (removed from service or returned to use)
